Dear Mr. Grimes: 
February 14, 1984 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-012 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Mr. Jud 
Porter, a potential candidate for a position on the Atascadero 
City Council. 
Mr. Porter is a copublisher of the Atascadero 
News, which is the city's only newspaper of general circu-
lation. 
For the past 4-5 years, the City has used the newspaper 
to publish its legal notices, although a formal contract has 
never been executed.ll Funds for the advertising are approved 
by the City Council as part of each year's budget. 
During the 
year, the City is billed by the newspaper for services rendered 
and the payments are eventually approved by the Council, as is 
the case with all of the City's bills. 
Mr. Porter has asked how 
the provisions of the Political Reform Act apply to these facts. 
DISCUSSION 
The provisions of the Political Reform Act will not prevent 
Mr. Porter from being a City Council candidate. 
If Mr. Porter 
is elected, the Act's provisions may affect his decisionmaking. 
Government Code Section 8710021 provides that no local public 
official shall make or participate in the making of a 
governmental decision if he knows or has reason to know that he 
has a financial interest. 
An official has a financial interest 
1/ This is the entire time that the City has published 
its notices. 
2/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 


Allen Grimes 
February 14, 1984 
Page 2 
in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is 
reasonaoly foreseeaole tnat tne decision will nave a material 
financial effect on: 
(a) 
Any business entity in whicn the pUblic 
official has a direct ••• investment worth more than 
one thousand dollars ($1,000). 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source or ~ncome • • • aggregating two 
hundred f~fty dollars ($250) or more in value provided 
to ••• tne public official within 12 months prior co 
tne time wnen tne decision is made. 
(d) 
Any bus~ness entity in which the public 
official ••• holds any position of management. 
(Sect~on 87103.) 
The Atascadero News is a business entity in which Mr. Porter 
nas both an investment interest and a position of management. 
The newspaper 
~s also a source of income to him. 
If ne is 
elected, Mr. Porter must disqualify nimself from making or 
participating in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that 
tne decision will nave a material financial effect on the 
Atascadero News. 
A deciSion's effect on the newspaper will be 
"material" if it will increase or decrease: 
of: 
(A) 
The annualized gross revenues by the lesser 
1. 
One hundred thousand dollars ($100,000); 
or 
2. 
One percent if the effect is one thousand 
dollars ($1,000) or more; or 
(B) 
Annual net income by tne lesser of: 
1. 
Fifty thousand dollars ($SO,OOO); or 
2. 
One half of one percent if the effect is 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more; or 


Allen Gr1mes 
February 14, 1984 
Page 3 
of: 
(C) 
Current assets or liabilities by tne lesser 
1. 
One hundred thousand dollars ($100,000): 
or 
2. 
One half of one percent if the effect is 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18702(b) (1) (A)-(C).) 
It is foreseeable that tne City Council's annual budget 
decision on tne allocation of money for advertising will have an 
effect on tne Atascadero News because the newspaper has always 
provided tne aavert1sing for the City and it appears that it 
will continue to do so in the future. 
Thus, if the annual 
amount of advertising money oeing approved is such that the 
receipt or loss of it by the Atascadero News will have a 
material financial effect on tne newspaper, Mr. Porter must 
disqualify nimself from participating in this aspect of the 
budget.V 
Once tne budget, including the advertising allocation, is 
approved, Mr. Porter wlll generally be able to approve the 
City's payment of bills submitted by tne newspaper. 
This is 
because, in most cases, the approval will be purely ministerial 
in nature ana will not const1tute "making or participating in 
tne making of a decision." 
(See the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. 
Adm. Code Section 18700 (d) (1) .) 
However, if a part icular 
payment 1S contested in some fasnion, the approval will no 
longer be ministerial and Mr. Porter may be required to 
disqualify nimself. 
I hope tnat I have clarified the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act for you. 
You indicated in your letter that you will 
be seeking the advice of the Attorney General's Office on the 
3/ 
See the enclosed copy of the COOk advice letter 
(A-83-l63), which descrioes now an offICIal can disqualify 
himself from one aspect of a budget. 


Allen Grimes 
February 14, 1984 
Page 4 
possible application of Section 1090. 
If you nave any 
additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 
(916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours, 
2~a~~1NUf/ 
Counsel 
Legal Division

as c adeN 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Attn: 
Diane Maura Fishburn 
Counsel, Legal Division 
Subject: 
Conflict of Interest Opinion 
Dear Ms. Fishburn: 
CITY ATTORNEY 
POST OFFICE BOX 749 
ATASCADERO. CALIFORNIA 93423 
PHONE: (80S) 466·5678 
-
POLICE DEPARTMENT 
POST OFFICE BOX 747 
ATASCADERO. CALIFORNIA 93423 
PHONE: (80S) 466·8600 
-
FIRE DEPARTMENT 
6005 LEWIS AVENUE 
ATASCADERO. CALIFORNIA 93422 
PHONE. (805) 466·2141 
I 
have 
received an urgent confidential inquiry in my capacity as City 
Attorney of the City of Atascadero concerning the possible application of 
the Fair Political Practices Act to a 
potential candidate for election to 
the City Council at the forthcoming election on June 
5, 1984. 
Due to the 
time factors 
involved, 
I 
would 
appreciate it if you could give me an 
advance oral opinion as to whether any conflict of interest exists in the 
situation, and then confirm later by confidential letter. 
The facts are these: 
The City of Atascadero is a general law city. It has 
called a general municipal election pursuant to state law for June 5, 1984. 
Three positions of the five-member Council (with an appointive Mayor) will 
be available for filling at the election. 
There is only one newspaper of general circulation in the City of Atasca-
dero (Govt Code § 6000), a biweekly newspaper, The Atascadero News. 
There 
are other newspapers of general circulation in other cities in the County 
of San 
Luis Obispo. 
Section 20169 
of the Public Contract Code requires 
bidding for publication of legal advertising in a city where there is more 
than one 
newspaper of general circulation printed and 
published in 
the 
city. 
When there is only one such newspaper, the section authorizes the 
legislative body of the city to contract with the newspaper without 
advertising for bids. 
The City of Atascadero 
has not been executing an 
annual contract for legal advertising with The Atascadero News. 
Funds for 
legal advertising are included in the annual city budget, which is approved 
by the Council. 
The City places its legal advertising 
in The Atascadero 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
January 16, 1984 
Page 2 
News 
and 
pays 
statements. All 
by the Council. 
the 
newspaper 
for 
services rendered upon receipt of the 
demands and payments of the 
City are eventually approved 
The potential candidate for a position on the 
Council is one of three co-
publishers of The Atascadero News and has a pecuniary proprietary interest 
in the newspaper. 
All this sums up to the question as 
to whether if the interested candidate 
were to run and be 
elected to the Council, and remain as a co-publisher of 
The Atascadero 
News, and the 
City continued to publish its legal ads 1n 
that newspaper, would there be a disqualifying conflict of interest? 
I perceive there is also a Government Code § 1090 aspect to this situation, 
and an 
opinion on 
that will have to 
be pursued 
through other channels. 
However, 
if you 
do have 
communication on matters of this kind with the 
Attorney General's Office, I would certainly appreciate any oral opinion 
that may 
be 
offered, 
and 
particularly whether the "Rule of Necessity" 
established by the Appellate Courts might apply under these circumstances. 
We do appreciate your assistance. 
Sincerely, 
ALLEN GRIMES 
City Attorney 
AG:fr 
, 
P.S. 
See opinion of the Legislative Counsel, 1972, A.J. 2913.

sections.question: If Mr. Porter, a co-publisher of the Atascadero News (the city's only newspaper of general circulation), is elected to the City Council, and the City continues to publish its legal notices in that newspaper, would there be a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion: The provisions of the Political Reform Act will not prevent Mr. Porter from being a City Council candidate. If Mr. Porter is elected, he must disqualify himself from participating in the City Council's annual budget decision regarding the allocation of money for advertising if the amount would have a material financial effect on the Atascadero News. Once the budget is approved, he will generally be able to approve the City's payment of bills submitted by the newspaper, as this is typically ministerial in nature, unless a payment is contested.

sections.question_synthetic: Does a potential city council candidate who is a co-publisher of the city's only newspaper face a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when the city publishes legal notices in that newspaper?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prevent Mr. Porter from being a candidate, but if elected, he must disqualify himself from budget decisions on advertising allocations that would materially affect the newspaper, though he may generally approve routine bill payments once the budget is set.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (January 16, 1984) and the FPPC response (February 14, 1984). Extracted information primarily from the FPPC response. Some OCR quality issues evident but content clearly discernible. Footnotes and regulatory citations included in conclusion text are relevant to the analysis.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If Mr. Porter, a co-publisher of the Atascadero News (the city's only newspaper of general circulation), is elected to the City Council, and the City continues to publish its legal notices in that newspaper, would there be a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The provisions of the Political Reform Act will not prevent Mr. Porter from being a City Council candidate. If Mr. Porter is elected, he must disqualify himself from participating in the City Council's annual budget decision regarding the allocation of money for advertising if the amount would have a material financial effect on the Atascadero News. Once the budget is approved, he will generally be able to approve the City's payment of bills submitted by the newspaper, as this is typically ministerial in nature, unless a payment is contested.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-84-012 addresses whether a potential city council candidate who is a co-publisher of the city's only newspaper faces a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding the city's publication of legal notices in that newspaper. The FPPC concludes that disqualification is required for budget decisions on advertising allocations with material financial effect, but routine bill approvals may be permitted as ministerial acts.
